summary:
Phase 1D — Notifications Engine. Backend: 13 pytest PASS + 1 skipped (offboarding e2e — skipped because the default offboarding flow needs a non-active employee candidate and the current ACME seed offers only test-suffixed users; the dispatch hook itself was code-reviewed and is identical to onboarding hook pattern). Frontend: ALL critical paths verified end-to-end. Backend pytest covered: GET /api/notifications list (returns array); /api/notifications/unread_count (int); POST /{id}/read + /read_all (read flag flips, count→0); GET /preferences auto-creates row with {in_app:true,email:true,push:false,digest:realtime}; PUT /preferences updates channels+mute_events+digest_frequency; tenant isolation (each user only sees their own recipient_user_id); leave apply → step1 approver (Rahul Verma, branch_manager) receives approval.pending with link='/app/approvals' and dedup_key='approval:{id}:step1'; after step1 approve → step2 approver (Priya Sharma, company_admin/HR) receives approval.pending; after step2 approve → requester (Aisha Khan) receives approval.approved; dedup — no duplicate dedup_keys across notifications collection; mute_events['approval.pending'] suppresses the event (unread_count unchanged), other events unaffected; channels.in_app=false suppresses inbox row persistence; RESEND_API_KEY not set in env — _send_email returns False silently and in-app still persists (verified end-to-end).

Frontend (Playwright, HR + Manager sessions):
  • Bell [data-testid=notifications-bell] visible in header for all logged-in users (HR overview + Approvals inbox).
  • Red counter [data-testid=notif-count] visible when unread>0 (observed '1' for manager after fresh leave submission).
  • Popover [data-testid=notif-popover] opens on bell click, lists last 10 notifications with title/body/timeAgo.
  • 'Mark all read' [data-testid=notif-readall] appears only when unread>0.
  • 'View all' [data-testid=notif-viewall] navigates to /app/notifications; 'Preferences' [data-testid=notif-prefs] navigates to /app/notification-prefs.
  • /app/notifications inbox: All/Unread filter buttons [data-testid=notif-filter-all|unread] + Mark-all-read button [data-testid=notif-readall-page] + Preferences link [data-testid=notif-prefs-link] + rows [data-testid=notif-row-{id}] with event tag + timestamp.
  • /app/notification-prefs: 3 channel cards (chan-in_app, chan-email, chan-push); push checkbox correctly disabled; 4 digest buttons (digest-realtime|daily|weekly|off); Save button [data-testid=prefs-save]; save flow executed (daily→realtime) without error.
  • END-TO-END: employee@acme.io submitted SL leave via API → logged in manager@acme.io in UI → bell badge showed '1' → clicked notification in popover → URL navigated to /app/approvals where the new PENDING Leave: Sick Leave row was visible in Inbox (66), with step chain Rahul Verma (step1 PENDING) → Priya Sharma (step2 PENDING).
critical_code_review_comments: notify.notify() swallows KeyError silently — if a data dict is missing a required template key, the event still fires but the title/body become the raw template strings (e.g. '{actor} needs your approval: {title}' literal). Consider logging the missing key at WARN so template gaps surface in dev.

notify._send_email — when RESEND_API_KEY is present but the HTTP call raises (timeout/TLS), the exception is caught and returns False with only a log.warning. A periodic failure-rate counter or an 'email_failures' collection would help HR ops notice silent email outage.

approvals_routes.create_approval_request — the notify hook is wrapped in a bare `except Exception: pass`, so any notification bug is silently swallowed. Add log.exception so issues are at least visible in backend logs. Same pattern should apply at the `/decide` hook.

notify.notify — prefs doc is loaded on every call; for high-throughput events (e.g. bulk onboarding 50 tasks), this is N extra DB reads. Cache per-request or batch in notify_many with a single $in lookup.

notifications_routes.list_mine — no pagination cursor; `limit=50` is the hard cap exposed via query. For users with >1000 notifs inbox, /app/notifications will get only 200 (page caps at 200 in frontend) with no next-page UI. Add `before_id` or offset param.
notifications_routes.mark_read returns {ok:true} even when no row matches the (id, recipient_user_id) filter. Should return 404 to prevent silent drift when a non-existent id is passed.

NotificationsBell.jsx — polling interval hard-coded to 60s; on tab blur the browser may throttle setInterval to much slower — consider adding visibilitychange listener to refresh immediately on focus.

Notifications.jsx inbox — event tag uses raw n.event.replace('.', ' · ') which works, but the 4 'new' notifications show verbatim 'leave · submitted' etc. A friendly-name map (same as EVENTS dict server-side) would read better for HR.

Notification mute UI gap — /app/notification-prefs shows channels + digest but NO way to view or edit mute_events list (backend supports it, frontend doesn't render it). Users currently cannot self-mute a noisy event type in UI; they can only do so via API.
notify.notify passes custom_title/custom_body params that no caller uses today — dead code path worth removing or documenting.

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 passing, 1 skipped for missing offboardable employee)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical paths — bell, popover, badge, inbox filters, preferences cards/digest, end-to-end click→navigate→approvals

test_credentials: hr@acme.io/Hr@12345, manager@acme.io/Manager@123, employee@acme.io/Employee@123 — all verified working
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_ prefixed leave requests (Aisha Khan, SL/EL/CL types) were created during tests to drive approval-chain notifications — each produces its own approval_request row + approval.pending notifs. These persist but do NOT affect any other tenant. Manager's notification_prefs row was toggled (mute, in_app=false) during PreferencesGating tests and RESTORED to defaults at end of each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Eight pytest suites now guard HRMS: test_hrms_backend.py, test_workflows.py, test_modules_phase0.py, test_phase1a_lifecycle.py, test_kb_phase1m.py, test_leave_phase1b.py, test_attendance_phase1c.py, test_notifications_phase1d.py. Phase 1D endpoints: /api/notifications (GET list, GET /unread_count, POST /{id}/read, POST /read_all, GET /preferences auto-create, PUT /preferences). Event names are in notify.EVENTS — 22 registered events across leave/approval/regularization/overtime/timesheet/onboarding/offboarding/profile/probation/birthday/work_anniversary/module/kb. Notification doc has dedup_key pattern 'approval:{req_id}:step{N}' for step-progression idempotency, and 'approval:{req_id}:final' for terminal decision. Frontend: NotificationsBell.jsx polls every 60s — set POLL_MS=3000 during manual QA if you want faster feedback. The bell element mount is in AppShell header so EVERY /app/* route shows it (verified on /app/hr and /app/approvals). RESEND_API_KEY is INTENTIONALLY UNSET — email sends log debug and return False; NOT a defect. Set RESEND_API_KEY env + `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` to activate real email sending. The one skipped test (TestOffboarding) can be re-enabled by seeding an employee whose user_id differs from employee@acme.io and status=active; the offboarding_routes.start_offboarding hook calls company_admins() and notify_many() with event='offboarding.initiated' (code confirmed via manual read).
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional defects. Two test-infrastructure tweaks made: (a) leave types CL/SL/EL balance may be consumed on repeated test runs — helper now tries SL→EL→CL in order and picks first with balance; (b) leave date must be a future Monday to avoid weekends + notice-day rejections — helper rolls forward to Monday after a 60-160 day random offset. Decision literal is 'approve'/'reject' (not 'approved'/'rejected') — already aligned with approvals schema.
